keynotes.title: BLIMP1 negatively regulates IL-2 signaling in T cells

keynote.title: BLIMP1 negatively regulates IL-2 signaling in T cells
keynote.core_problem: Understanding the role of BLIMP1 in regulating IL-2 signaling, which is crucial for immune homeostasis and the balance between effector and regulatory T (Treg) cells.
contributions: Identification of BLIMP1 as a negative regulator of IL-2 signaling through genome-wide CRISPR-knockout screening.
Demonstration that manipulating BLIMP1 levels can modulate IL-2 signaling, suggesting potential therapeutic applications.
methods: Genome-wide CRISPR-knockout screening in IL-2–dependent cells derived from a patient with adult T cell leukemia (ATL).
Overexpression and knockout experiments of Prdm1/PRDM1 in mouse and human CD4+ T cells and Treg cells.
Assessment of IL-2 signaling in T cells from influenza-infected mice and during adoptive T cell transfer–induced colitis.
experiments_or_evidence: Enrichment of single guide RNAs targeting PRDM1 in the CRISPR screen indicating its role in IL-2 signaling.
Overexpressing Prdm1 down-regulated IL-2 signaling, while Prdm1-deficiency enhanced IL-2 signaling in mouse CD4+ T cells and Treg cells.
Increased IL-2 signaling in T cells from influenza-infected mice and during adoptive T cell transfer–induced colitis when Prdm1 was deficient.
Deleting PRDM1 in human CD4+ T cells and Treg cells increased IL-2 signaling.
CD4+ T cells from patients with ATL expressed less BLIMP1 and had enhanced IL-2 signaling, and overexpressing PRDM1 in ATL cells suppressed IL-2 signaling.
assumptions: BLIMP1's known function in inhibiting IL-2 production by T cells.

gap_signals.text: The role of BLIMP1 in IL-2 signaling remains unknown before this study.

datasets_or_materials: IL-2–dependent cells derived from a patient with adult T cell leukemia (ATL).
Mouse CD4+ T cells and Treg cells.
Human CD4+ T cells and Treg cells.
CD4+ T cells from patients with ATL.

important_claims.claim: BLIMP1 inhibits IL-2 signaling in T cells.
important_claims.evidence: Overexpressing Prdm1 down-regulated IL-2 signaling, whereas Prdm1-deficiency enhanced IL-2 signaling in mouse CD4+ T cells and Treg cells. Deleting PRDM1 in human CD4+ T cells and Treg cells also increased IL-2 signaling.

important_claims.claim: Manipulating BLIMP1 could have therapeutic potential.
important_claims.evidence: CD4+ T cells from patients with ATL expressed less BLIMP1 and had enhanced IL-2 signaling, whereas overexpressing PRDM1 in ATL cells suppressed IL-2 signaling.

steps.claim: Prdm1 overexpression leads to down-regulation of IL-2 signaling.
steps.evidence: Overexpressing Prdm1 down-regulated IL-2 signaling in mouse CD4+ T cells and Treg cells.

causal_chains.outcome: Down-regulation of IL-2 signaling
observables: Reduced IL-2 signaling in T cells

steps.claim: Prdm1 deficiency leads to enhancement of IL-2 signaling.
steps.evidence: Prdm1-deficiency enhanced IL-2 signaling in mouse CD4+ T cells and Treg cells, and deleting PRDM1 in human CD4+ T cells and Treg cells also increased IL-2 signaling.

causal_chains.outcome: Enhancement of IL-2 signaling
observables: Increased IL-2 signaling in T cells

keynote.reuse_value_for_research: This study provides a new target (BLIMP1) for modulating IL-2 signaling, which could be explored in further research on immune regulation and potential therapeutic interventions in diseases involving T cell dysfunction.

keynotes.title: Differential Roles of IL-2 Signaling in Developing versus Mature Tregs

keynote.title: Differential Roles of IL-2 Signaling in Developing versus Mature Tregs
keynote.core_problem: Understanding the role of IL-2 signaling in the development and maintenance of Foxp3+ regulatory T cells (Tregs).
contributions: Clarifying the differential roles of IL-2 in developing and mature Tregs.
Demonstrating that IL-2 is dispensable for maintaining lineage stability in mature Tregs but essential for their survival and suppressor function.
methods: Generating mice with inducible deletion of CD25, the main ligand-binding subunit of the IL-2 receptor, from Tregs after thymic development.
experiments_or_evidence: Observing the persistence of CD25-deleted Tregs in vivo using IL-7.
Noting defects in glycolytic metabolism and suppressor function following CD25 deletion.

gap_signals.text: It has been unclear whether continuing IL-2 signals are needed to maintain lineage stability, survival, and suppressor function in mature Tregs.

important_claims.claim: IL-2 is dispensable for maintaining lineage stability in mature Tregs.
important_claims.evidence: In contrast to Treg development, we find that IL-2 is dispensable for maintaining lineage stability in mature Tregs.

important_claims.claim: Continuous IL-2 signaling is required for the survival and suppressor function of mature Tregs.
important_claims.evidence: Continuous IL-2 signaling is required for the survival and suppressor function of mature Tregs, but not for lineage stability.

causal_chains.trigger: Deletion of CD25 in mature Tregs

steps.claim: IL-2 is dispensable for maintaining lineage stability in mature Tregs.
steps.evidence: In contrast to Treg development, we find that IL-2 is dispensable for maintaining lineage stability in mature Tregs.

steps.claim: Continuous IL-2 signaling is needed for long-term Treg survival.
steps.evidence: Although continuous IL-2 signaling is needed for long-term Treg survival, CD25-deleted Tregs may persist for several weeks in vivo using IL-7.

steps.claim: Defects in glycolytic metabolism and suppressor function occur following CD25 deletion.
steps.evidence: We also observe defects in glycolytic metabolism and suppressor function following CD25 deletion.

causal_chains.outcome: Mature Tregs require continuous IL-2 signaling to maintain survival and suppressor function, but not to maintain lineage stability.
observables: Persistence of CD25-deleted Tregs in vivo using IL-7
Defects in glycolytic metabolism and suppressor function
interventions: Inducible deletion of CD25 in Tregs after thymic development

keynote.reuse_value_for_research: This study provides a clear distinction between the roles of IL-2 in developing and mature Tregs, which can guide future research on Treg biology and therapeutic strategies involving Treg manipulation.

keynotes.title: Regulation of Treg cells by cytokine signaling and co-stimulatory molecules

keynote.title: Regulation of Treg cells by cytokine signaling and co-stimulatory molecules
keynote.core_problem: Understanding the regulation of CD4+CD25+Foxp3+ regulatory T cells (Tregs) by cytokines and co-stimulatory molecules, and their role in maintaining immune homeostasis and preventing excessive immune responses.
contributions: Explores the signaling pathways of cytokines that regulate Treg cells, including TGF-β, IL-2, IL-10, and IL-35, which enhance the differentiation and immunosuppressive capabilities of Tregs.
Examines how signals mediated by IL-6 and TNF-α can undermine Treg suppressive functions or reprogram them into effector T cells.
Focuses on the role of co-stimulatory molecules, particularly CTLA-4 and PD-1, in regulating the differentiation, function, and survival of Tregs.
experiments_or_evidence: Studies in EBI3−/− and p35−/− mice (IL-35 subunit knockout models) show that IL-35 is important for maintaining Treg function.
Co-culture experiments with multiple myeloma (MM) cells and bone marrow stromal cells demonstrate that IL-6 can transform Tregs into Th17 cells.
assumptions: Tregs play a crucial role in inhibiting the activity of immune cells, ensuring the balance of the immune system, and preventing autoimmune diseases.
limitations: The review does not provide detailed experimental methods, focusing instead on summarizing existing research and theoretical understanding.

gap_signals.text: The review does not provide detailed experimental methods, focusing instead on summarizing existing research and theoretical understanding.
The study of Treg signaling pathways provides a theoretical basis for immune balance and the treatment of autoimmune diseases.

important_claims.claim: Cytokines such as TGF-β, IL-2, IL-10, and IL-35 foster the differentiation and enhance the immunosuppressive capabilities of Tregs.
important_claims.evidence: This review explores the signaling pathways of the cytokines that regulate Treg cells, including transforming growth factor beta (TGF-β), interleukin (IL)-2, IL-10, and IL-35, which foster the differentiation and enhance the immunosuppressive capabilities of Tregs.

important_claims.claim: Signals mediated by IL-6 and TNF-α can undermine Treg suppressive functions or drive their reprogramming into effector T cells.
important_claims.evidence: It also examines how, conversely, signals mediated by IL-6 and tumor necrosis factor -alpha (TNF-α) can undermine Treg suppressive functions or even drive their reprogramming into effector T cells.

steps.claim: IL-35 deficiency leads to a decrease in Foxp3 expression.
steps.evidence: The function of Tregs proved effective in EBI3−/−and p35−/− mice, that is, IL-35 subunit knockout models, suggesting that IL-35 plays an important role in maintaining the function of Tregs.

steps.claim: Decrease in Foxp3 expression causes CD4+ T cells to differentiate into Th17 cells instead of Tregs.
steps.evidence: The immunosuppressive capacity of Tregs in EBI3−/−and p35−/− cells, followed by a decrease in Foxp3 expression, thus causing CD4+ T cells to differentiate into Th17 cells instead of Tregs.

causal_chains.outcome: Impaired Treg function and increased Th17 cell differentiation.

steps.claim: IL-6 transforms Tregs into Th17 cells.
steps.evidence: Another study reported that when co-cultured with multiple myeloma (MM) cells, bone marrow stromal cells could secrete IL-6 and thereby transform Tregs into Th17 cells, a finding further verified in animal models.

causal_chains.outcome: Transformation of Tregs into Th17 cells.
observables: Transformation of Tregs into Th17 cells

keynote.reuse_value_for_research: The review provides a comprehensive overview of the regulation of Tregs by cytokines and co-stimulatory molecules, which can serve as a foundation for future research in developing Treg-based immunotherapies for autoimmune diseases, transplant rejection, and cancer.

keynotes.title: CD25-Treg-depleting antibodies preserving IL-2 signaling on effector T cells enhance effector activation and antitumor immunity

keynote.title: CD25-Treg-depleting antibodies preserving IL-2 signaling on effector T cells enhance effector activation and antitumor immunity
keynote.core_problem: Intratumoral regulatory T (Treg) cell abundance associates with diminished antitumor immunity and poor prognosis in human cancers. Anti-human CD25 antibodies have failed to deliver clinical responses against solid tumors due to bystander IL-2 receptor signaling blockade on effector T cells, which limits their antitumor activity.
contributions: Demonstration of potent single-agent activity of anti-CD25 antibodies optimized to deplete Treg cells while preserving IL-2-STAT5 signaling on effector T cells, showing synergy with immune checkpoint blockade in vivo.

Development of a novel non-IL-2-blocking human anti-CD25NIB (RG6292) antibody that demonstrates efficient Treg cell depletion with no overt immune-related toxicities in both nonhuman primates and humanized mouse models.
methods: In vitro and in vivo testing of anti-CD25 antibodies, including binding affinity, STAT5 phosphorylation, and Treg depletion assays.
Assessment of the impact of these antibodies on Treg and effector T cell populations in tumor-bearing mice and human PBMCs.
Evaluation of RG6292 in nonhuman primates and humanized mouse models for Treg depletion and effector T cell activation.
experiments_or_evidence: In vitro experiments demonstrating that RG6292 depletes Treg cells without blocking IL-2-STAT5 signaling on effector T cells.
In vivo studies in C57BL6 mice injected with MCA205 tumor cells, showing that RG6292 promotes Treg depletion and enhances effector T cell activation.
Pre-clinical evaluation in nonhuman primates and humanized mouse models, showing efficient Treg depletion with no overt immune-related toxicities.
assumptions: The final effector of Treg depletion in vivo in humans would likely differ between tumour types, depending on the presence of innate effector subsets.
limitations: The study did not formally assess whether macrophages or NK cells were the mediators of Treg depletion.
The impact of RG6292 on different tumor types and the presence of innate effector subsets was not fully explored.

gap_signals.text: The study did not formally assess whether macrophages or NK cells were the mediators of Treg depletion.
The impact of RG6292 on different tumor types and the presence of innate effector subsets was not fully explored.
The final effector of Treg depletion in vivo in humans would likely differ between tumour types, depending on the presence of innate effector subsets.

datasets_or_materials: Human PBMCs, frozen dissociated tumor cells from Conversant Bio, and polyclonally activated human PBMCs.
MCA205 and MC38 tumor cell lines in C57BL6 mice.
Nonhuman primate and humanized mouse models.

important_claims.claim: Anti-CD25 antibodies optimized to deplete Treg cells while preserving IL-2-STAT5 signaling on effector T cells show potent single-agent activity and synergy with immune checkpoint blockade in vivo.
important_claims.evidence: In vivo studies in C57BL6 mice injected with MCA205 tumor cells, showing that RG6292 promotes Treg depletion and enhances effector T cell activation, and pre-clinical evaluation in nonhuman primates and humanized mouse models, showing efficient Treg depletion with no overt immune-related toxicities.

important_claims.claim: RG6292 effectively depletes human Treg while preserving IL-2R signaling on Teff, whereas Daclizumab reduces both IL-2R signaling and T cell activation.
important_claims.evidence: In vitro experiments demonstrating that RG6292 depletes Treg cells without blocking IL-2-STAT5 signaling on effector T cells, and Daclizumab ablates STAT5 expressing regulatory T cells and not that of activated CD8 and CD4 effector T cells when present during polyclonal activation of human PBMCs.

causal_chains.trigger: Use of anti-CD25 antibodies optimized to deplete Treg cells while preserving IL-2-STAT5 signaling on effector T cells

steps.claim: Efficient Treg cell depletion is achieved
steps.evidence: In vivo studies in C57BL6 mice injected with MCA205 tumor cells, showing that RG6292 promotes Treg depletion.

steps.claim: Preservation of IL-2-STAT5 signaling on effector T cells
steps.evidence: In vitro experiments demonstrating that RG6292 depletes Treg cells without blocking IL-2-STAT5 signaling on effector T cells.

steps.claim: Enhanced effector T cell activation and antitumor immunity
steps.evidence: In vivo studies in C57BL6 mice injected with MCA205 tumor cells, showing that RG6292 enhances effector T cell activation and shows synergy with immune checkpoint blockade.

causal_chains.outcome: Potent single-agent activity and synergy with immune checkpoint blockade in vivo

keynote.reuse_value_for_research: The development of RG6292 and its demonstrated efficacy in depleting Treg cells while preserving IL-2-STAT5 signaling on effector T cells provides a valuable tool for enhancing antitumor immunity. The data support further clinical development and evaluation of new combination therapies incorporating non-IL-2-blocking anti-CD25 antibodies in clinical studies.

keynotes.title: OX40 Costimulation Inhibits Foxp3 Expression and Treg Induction via BATF3-Dependent and Independent Mechanisms

keynote.title: OX40 Costimulation Inhibits Foxp3 Expression and Treg Induction via BATF3-Dependent and Independent Mechanisms
keynote.core_problem: Understanding the mechanisms by which OX40 signaling inhibits Foxp3 expression and Treg induction in naive CD4+ T cells.
contributions: Identification of two distinct molecular pathways (BATF3/BATF and AKT-mTOR) through which OX40 inhibits Foxp3 expression and Treg induction.
methods: Experimental analysis of OX40 costimulation on naive CD4+ T cells, including gene expression, chromatin configuration, and pathway activation studies.
experiments_or_evidence: OX40 upregulates BATF3 and BATF, leading to a closed chromatin configuration that represses Foxp3 expression in a Sirt1/7-dependent manner.
OX40 activates the AKT-mTOR pathway, especially in the absence of BATF3 and BATF, to inhibit Foxp3 induction, mediated by phosphorylation and nuclear exclusion of Foxo1.
assumptions: Naive CD4+ T cells can be converted to Foxp3+ T regulatory cells (iTregs) in the periphery, where Foxp3 gene expression is central to Treg differentiation.

gap_signals.text: The underlying mechanisms by which OX40 signaling inhibits Foxp3 expression and Treg induction remain poorly defined.

important_claims.evidence: Specifically, OX40 upregulates BATF3 and BATF, which produce a closed chromatin configuration to repress Foxp3 expression in a Sirt1/7-dependent manner.

important_claims.claim: OX40 can also activate the AKT-mTOR pathway, especially in the absence of BATF3 and BATF, to inhibit Foxp3 induction, and this is mediated by phosphorylation and nuclear exclusion of the transcription factor Foxo1.

keynotes.title: Regulatory T cells and their role in inflammatory bowel disease: molecular targets, therapeutic strategies and translational advances.

keynote.title: Regulatory T cells and their role in inflammatory bowel disease: molecular targets, therapeutic strategies and translational advances
keynote.core_problem: Treg dysfunction in Inflammatory Bowel Disease (IBD) leading to loss of lineage stability, impaired suppressive function, and disrupted balance with pro-inflammatory Th17 cells.
contributions: Identification of key pathways controlling Treg differentiation, epigenetic maintenance, and metabolic signaling in IBD.
Evaluation of pharmacological strategies aimed at restoring immune balance in IBD.
Discussion of ongoing clinical trials and future opportunities for precision therapy in IBD.
experiments_or_evidence: Genetic and translational evidence supporting the role of Tregs in IBD.
Ongoing clinical trials and omics-driven patient stratification.
assumptions: Treg dysfunction is a significant factor in the pathogenesis of IBD.
limitations: The review does not provide detailed experimental data or specific results from clinical trials.

gap_signals.text: The review does not provide detailed experimental data or specific results from clinical trials.
Future opportunities involving omics-driven patient stratification, targeted delivery platforms, and chimeric antigen receptor (CAR)-Treg technologies.

important_claims.evidence: This dysfunction is marked by loss of lineage stability, impaired suppressive function, and a disrupted balance with pro-inflammatory T helper type 17 (Th17) cells.

important_claims.claim: Key pathways such as IL-2/STAT5, FOXP3/CNS2, mTOR, and RORγt are genetically linked to IBD susceptibility and offer viable targets for therapeutic development.
important_claims.evidence: These include interleukin-2/signal transducer and activator of transcription 5 (IL-2/STAT5), forkhead box P3/conserved non-coding DNA sequences 2 (FOXP3/CNS2), mechanistic target of rapamycin (mTOR), and retinoic acid-related orphan receptor gamma t (RORγt) pathways, several of which are genetically linked to IBD susceptibility and offer viable targets for therapeutic development.

keynotes.title: Catalpol enhances mTORC1/HIF-1α/PDHK1-mediated oxidative metabolism that leads to promote the differentiation of Treg cells in RA

keynote.title: Catalpol enhances mTORC1/HIF-1α/PDHK1-mediated oxidative metabolism that leads to promote the differentiation of Treg cells in RA
keynote.core_problem: Rheumatoid arthritis (RA) is a chronic autoimmune disease where regulatory T (Treg) cell dysfunction contributes to its pathogenesis, and the mechanisms regulating Treg differentiation are not fully understood.
contributions: Investigated the effects of Catalpol (CAT) on Treg differentiation and its underlying mechanisms using a CIA mouse model.
methods: Used a CIA mouse model with animals randomly allocated to CIA, CIA+CAT, and No CIA groups.
Assessed synovial pathology by HE staining.
Differentiated naive CD4+ T cells into Tregs in vitro and treated with CAT.
Performed molecular docking to predict CAT-target interactions.
Used the mTORC1 activator NV5138 alongside CAT for intervention.
Analyzed Treg proportions and mitochondrial membrane potential by flow cytometry.
Measured IL-10 levels by ELISA.
Detected NAD+/NADH by assay kit.
Determined mRNA expression of FOXP3, mTOR, Raptor, HIF-1α, and PDHK1 by RT-qPCR.
Assessed protein expression by Western blotting.
experiments_or_evidence: CAT alleviated joint symptoms in CIA mice.
CAT promoted Treg differentiation both in vivo and in vitro.
CAT increased mitochondrial membrane potential, NAD+/NADH, and acetyl-CoA levels in Tregs.
CAT downregulated HIF-1α and PDHK1 mRNA and inhibited p-P70S6K/P70S6K, p-4EBP1/4EBP1, HIF-1α, and PDHK1 protein expression.
Treatment with NV5138 reduced Treg differentiation and oxidative metabolism, effects which were reversed by CAT.
assumptions: The CIA mouse model is a valid representation of human RA.
The in vitro Treg differentiation model reflects in vivo conditions.
limitations: The study primarily focuses on the CIA mouse model and may not fully represent all aspects of human RA.
Further studies are needed to confirm the findings in human subjects.

gap_signals.text: Further studies are needed to confirm the findings in human subjects.
The study primarily focuses on the CIA mouse model and may not fully represent all aspects of human RA.
The mechanisms regulating Treg differentiation in RA remain unclear.

code_or_implementation: Molecular docking software for predicting CAT-target interactions

important_claims.claim: CAT promotes Treg differentiation and exerts anti-RA effects through inhibition of the mTORC1/HIF-1α/PDHK1 signaling pathway and enhancement of pyruvate aerobic oxidation.
important_claims.evidence: CAT alleviated joint symptoms in CIA mice, promoted Treg differentiation, and increased mitochondrial membrane potential, NAD+/NADH, and acetyl-CoA levels. CAT also downregulated HIF-1α and PDHK1 mRNA and inhibited related protein expressions. Treatment with NV5138 reduced Treg differentiation and oxidative metabolism, effects which were reversed by CAT.

steps.claim: CAT inhibits the mTORC1/HIF-1α/PDHK1 signaling pathway
steps.evidence: CAT downregulated HIF-1α and PDHK1 mRNA and inhibited p-P70S6K/P70S6K, p-4EBP1/4EBP1, HIF-1α, and PDHK1 protein expression.

steps.claim: Inhibition of the mTORC1/HIF-1α/PDHK1 pathway enhances pyruvate aerobic oxidation
steps.evidence: CAT increased mitochondrial membrane potential, NAD+/NADH, and acetyl-CoA levels in Tregs.

steps.claim: Enhanced pyruvate aerobic oxidation promotes Treg differentiation
steps.evidence: CAT promoted Treg differentiation both in vivo and in vitro.

causal_chains.outcome: CAT exerts anti-RA effects
observables: Alleviation of joint symptoms in CIA mice
Increased mitochondrial membrane potential, NAD+/NADH, and acetyl-CoA levels

keynote.reuse_value_for_research: The study provides a detailed mechanistic understanding of how Catalpol affects Treg differentiation and RA, which can be valuable for developing new therapeutic strategies for RA and other autoimmune diseases. The methods and results can be used as a reference for further research on the role of metabolic pathways in immune regulation.

keynotes.title: CD226 maintains regulatory T cell phenotype stability and metabolism by the mTOR/Myc pathway under inflammatory conditions

keynote.title: CD226 maintains regulatory T cell phenotype stability and metabolism by the mTOR/Myc pathway under inflammatory conditions
keynote.core_problem: Understanding the role of CD226 in maintaining the stability and metabolic function of regulatory T (Treg) cells, especially under inflammatory conditions.
contributions: Demonstrated that CD226 is crucial for maintaining Treg cell stability and metabolism under inflammatory conditions.
Showed that conditional deletion of CD226 within Foxp3+ cells exacerbates symptoms in murine graft versus host disease models.
Identified that CD226-deficient Treg cells exhibit reduced oxidative phosphorylation and increased glycolysis rates, regulated by the AMPK/mTOR/Myc pathway.
methods: Conditional deletion of CD226 within Foxp3+ cells
Analysis of Treg cell function and phenotype in inflammatory disease models
experiments_or_evidence: Conditional deletion of CD226 within Foxp3+ cells exacerbated symptoms in murine graft versus host disease models.
Treg cell-specific deletion of CD226 increased the Treg cell percentage in immune organs but weakened their immunosuppressive function with a T helper 1-like phenotype conversion under inflammation.
Inhibition of Myc signaling restored the impaired functions of CD226-deficient Treg cells in an inflammatory disease model of colitis.
assumptions: The role of CD226 in Treg cell function and metabolism is context-dependent, particularly under inflammatory conditions.
limitations: The study primarily focuses on murine models, and the translation to human Treg cells and inflammatory diseases may require further investigation.

gap_signals.text: The study primarily focuses on murine models, and the translation to human Treg cells and inflammatory diseases may require further investigation.
Translation of findings from murine models to human Treg cells and inflammatory diseases.

important_claims.evidence: Conditional deletion of CD226 within Foxp3+ cells exacerbates symptoms in murine graft versus host disease models.

important_claims.claim: CD226-deficient Treg cells exhibit reduced oxidative phosphorylation and increased glycolysis rates, regulated by the AMPK/mTOR/Myc pathway.
important_claims.evidence: Inhibition of Myc signaling restores the impaired functions of CD226-deficient Treg cells in an inflammatory disease model of colitis.

keynotes.title: Mettl14 sustains FOXP3 expression to promote the differentiation and functions of induced-regulatory T cells via the mTOR signaling pathway.

keynote.title: Mettl14 sustains FOXP3 expression to promote the differentiation and functions of induced-regulatory T cells via the mTOR signaling pathway
keynote.core_problem: Induced regulatory T cells (iTregs) fail to maintain FoxP3 expression and suppressive activity, limiting their clinical application in treating autoimmune diseases and preventing transplant rejection.
contributions: Demonstrated that METTL14 is critical for the differentiation and suppressive function of iTregs
Showed that METTL14 deficiency disrupts iTreg differentiation and reduces FoxP3 expression
Identified that reduced METTL14 leads to activation of the mTOR pathway, which modulates the suppressive function of iTregs
methods: Generated iTregs from CD4+ naïve T cells under iTreg-polarizing conditions
Knocked down METTL14 expression using siRNA-METTL14 interference in CD4+ naïve T cells
Measured the expression of FoxP3 and inflammatory cytokines (IFN-γ and IL-17a) in cultured iTregs
Performed colitis development assays in Rag1-/- mice and CFSE assays to evaluate the suppressive function of METTL14-deficient iTregs
Analyzed the mTOR pathway by measuring p-mTOR and p-p70S6K levels
experiments_or_evidence: Expression of METTL14 was increased in iTregs compared with CD4+ naïve T cells
Mettl14 deficiency resulted in limited FoxP3 expression and upregulated inflammatory cytokines (IFN-γ and IL-17a)

keynotes.title: Response to: ‘Circulating regulatory T cells were absolutely decreased in dermatomyositis/polymyositis patients and restored by low-dose IL-2’ by Zhang et al

keynotes.title: Selective activation and expansion of regulatory T cells using lipid encapsulated mRNA encoding a long-acting IL-2 mutein

keynote.title: Selective activation and expansion of regulatory T cells using lipid encapsulated mRNA encoding a long-acting IL-2 mutein
keynote.core_problem: IL-2 is critical for regulatory T cell (Treg) function, but even at low doses, it can activate proinflammatory, non-Treg T cells. Improving the specificity of IL-2 towards Tregs may be beneficial for treating autoimmune diseases.
contributions: Development of a half-life-extended human IL-2 mutein (HSA-IL2m) with mutations promoting reliance on IL-2Rα, delivered as lipid-encapsulated mRNA nanoparticles.
Demonstration of selective activation and expansion of Tregs in mice and non-human primates using HSA-IL2m.
Reduction in disease severity in mouse models of acute graft versus host disease and experimental autoimmune encephalomyelitis.
methods: Subcutaneous delivery of lipid-encapsulated mRNA nanoparticles encoding HSA-IL2m or HSA-IL2wt to C57BL/6 mice and cynomolgus monkeys.
Assessment of STAT5 phosphorylation in Tregs and other T cell subsets in response to HSA-IL2m and HSA-IL2wt in vitro.
Evaluation of Treg expansion and activation in vivo, and assessment of disease severity in mouse models of acute graft versus host disease and experimental autoimmune encephalomyelitis.
experiments_or_evidence: In vitro, HSA-IL2m induced STAT5 phosphorylation exclusively in Tregs, while HSA-IL2wt induced STAT5 phosphorylation in Tregs, NK, and conventional T cells.
In vivo, HSA-IL2m led to sustained Treg expansion and activation in both mice and cynomolgus monkeys, with a prolonged effect compared to HSA-IL2wt.
Treatment with HSA-IL2m reduced disease severity in mouse models of acute graft versus host disease and experimental autoimmune encephalomyelitis.
assumptions: The engineered IL-2 mutein (HSA-IL2m) will have increased specificity for Tregs due to its mutations promoting reliance on IL-2Rα.
The use of lipid-encapsulated mRNA nanoparticles for delivering HSA-IL2m will effectively target and expand Tregs in vivo.

keynote.core_problem: Autoimmune and rheumatic diseases exhibit disturbances in Treg biology, leading to an imbalance between protective and pathogenic immune cells. A relative deficiency of IL-2 during disease pathogenesis can further disturb Treg homeostasis, amplifying chronic inflammation.

keynote.core_problem: Understanding the cellular neighborhoods and spatial compartmentalization that shape microorganism-reactive Treg cell function in the gut, which is critical for re-establishing tolerance following dysfunction, such as in inflammatory disorders.

Provided insights into receptor–ligand pairs likely governing the interaction between CD206+ macrophages and eTreg cells.

Spatial transcriptomics

keynote.core_problem: The role and function of ZFP36 family RNA binding proteins (RBP) in regulatory T (Treg) cells, specifically in maintaining immune homeostasis, is not well understood.
contributions: Identified the role of Zfp36l1 and Zfp36l2 in Treg cells to maintain immune homeostasis.

sub_hypotheses.source_objective:
在定义明确的生理状态、扰动条件（如感染、自身抗原、肿瘤）和干预窗口下，个体的遗传背景、细胞组成、细胞内在状态、局部微环境（如细胞因子、代谢物）以及外部扰动，是否能够以可重复、可量化、因果上可解释的方式预测：
1. 特定免疫细胞亚群（如Treg, Th17, CD8+ T）的稳态增殖、分化与凋亡动态；
2. 关键细胞因子或代谢信号通路（如IL-2, TGF-β, mTOR）的阈值效应与反馈调节；
3. 免疫调节干预（如低剂量IL-2、检查点激动剂/拮抗剂）是否能够重建而非简单地增强或抑制免疫应答；
4. 上述稳态调节规则在不同遗传背景、组织微环境（屏障组织vs.循环系统）、年龄、炎症状态及跨物种（小鼠模型vs.人类）迁移时是否仍成立。

retrieval_constraints: Use semantic_scholar, PubMed, bioRxiv/medRxiv; clearly label preprints as not peer-reviewed. Import at least 15 quality-screened papers covering each sub-hypothesis. Exclude descriptive omics papers without functional validation, single immortalized cell line studies, and purely computational papers without human or animal model validation.

evidence_window_alerts.action: Record the independent preprint-search gap, but do not block causal-gap exploration or hypothesis generation while this temporary policy is active.

objective_decomposition.objective:
在定义明确的生理状态、扰动条件（如感染、自身抗原、肿瘤）和干预窗口下，个体的遗传背景、细胞组成、细胞内在状态、局部微环境（如细胞因子、代谢物）以及外部扰动，是否能够以可重复、可量化、因果上可解释的方式预测：
1. 特定免疫细胞亚群（如Treg, Th17, CD8+ T）的稳态增殖、分化与凋亡动态；
2. 关键细胞因子或代谢信号通路（如IL-2, TGF-β, mTOR）的阈值效应与反馈调节；
3. 免疫调节干预（如低剂量IL-2、检查点激动剂/拮抗剂）是否能够重建而非简单地增强或抑制免疫应答；
4. 上述稳态调节规则在不同遗传背景、组织微环境（屏障组织vs.循环系统）、年龄、炎症状态及跨物种（小鼠模型vs.人类）迁移时是否仍成立。

objective_decomposition.research_brief:
创建并运行一个 AutoGen 科研闭环验证项目。

项目标题：
How is immune homeostasis maintained and regulated?
从动态平衡、个体异质性到可干预靶点的免疫系统稳态因果验证闭环

领域：
Immunology / Systems Biology / T Cell Biology / Cytokine Signaling /
Regulatory T Cells (Tregs) / Immune Checkpoints / Inflammation /
Autoimmunity / Tumor Immunology / Single-cell Multi-omics /
Functional Assays / Mathematical Modeling / Translational Medicine

研究目标：
不要泛泛讨论 免疫稳态对健康至关重要。围绕以下可分解、可证伪的生物学与转化问题开展验证：

在定义明确的生理状态、扰动条件（如感染、自身抗原、肿瘤）和干预窗口下，个体的遗传背景、细胞组成、细胞内在状态、局部微环境（如细胞因子、代谢物）以及外部扰动，是否能够以可重复、可量化、因果上可解释的方式预测：

1. 特定免疫细胞亚群（如Treg, Th17, CD8+ T）的稳态增殖、分化与凋亡动态；
2. 关键细胞因子或代谢信号通路（如IL-2, TGF-β, mTOR）的阈值效应与反馈调节；
3. 免疫调节干预（如低剂量IL-2、检查点激动剂/拮抗剂）是否能够重建而非简单地增强或抑制免疫应答；
4. 上述稳态调节规则在不同遗传背景、组织微环境（屏障组织vs.循环系统）、年龄、炎症状态及跨物种（小鼠模型vs.人类）迁移时是否仍成立。

禁止将细胞亚群频率、细胞因子浓度或基因表达差异直接解释为稳态调节机制；
禁止将体外刺激或小鼠模型的结论直接外推为人类体内系统层面的稳态调节规律；
禁止将系统复杂性误判为不可知论，即不可知论不应阻碍对关键调节节点的假设驱动研究。

执行完整闭环：
Boxue → Objective Decomposition → ZhiZhi → TanXi → Socrates →
MingLi → YanZhen → DuZhi → BianLun → MingLi 修订 → YanZhen 复审。


## Boxue：Objective Decomposition

在任何宽泛检索前，先运行 decompose_research_objective。
输出不是一组研究方向，而是独立的、可被推翻的子假设。


## ZhiZhi：文献与证据知识库要求

数据源与质量边界

- 主要使用 semantic_scholar、PubMed、bioRxiv/medRxiv；免疫学前沿预印本需明确标注未经同行评审。
- 分层导入至少 15 篇经质量筛选的文献；数量不是目标，必须覆盖每个已选子假设的证据窗口。
- 对摘要缺失、unknown 字段、来源可疑或 requires_human_review 的记录，执行提取修复、降权或停留在背景层，不得作为机制合同的唯一证据。

- 不得制造论文、临床试验、效应量、患者结局或指南推荐。

P0–P4 分层

P0 / L3：近期预印本
  - 系统免疫学、单细胞多组学、免疫调节、自身免疫、肿瘤免疫
  - 要求标注未经同行评审；检索失败必须显式标记证据不足

P1 / L2：近2年高质量原始研究
  - 人体免疫监测研究、人类遗传学（如GWAS）、针对患者的干预研究
  - 功能验证实验（如体外共培养、类器官）
  - 纵向随访研究（如感染、疫苗接种、自身免疫病）

P2 / L4：机制、实验与理论证据
  - 细胞信号转导（如TCR、共刺激、细胞因子受体）
  - 代谢与表观遗传重编程
  - 经典免疫学实验（如基因敲除小鼠、过继转移、抗体阻断）

P3 / L0：系统综述、临床指南、方法学综述
  - 免疫学教科书、领域权威综述、调节性T细胞/Th17/检查点综述
  - 因果推断、生物标志物验证指南

P4 / L1：奠基研究与历史脉络
  - 免疫耐受、克隆选择、Treg发现等奠基性工作
  - 仅用于历史和机制脉络，不得替代当前直接证据

必须覆盖的子空间

- 调节性T细胞（Treg）的发育、维持和功能可塑性；
- 效应T细胞（Teff）与Treg之间的动态平衡（包括关键细胞因子IL-2, TGF-β）；
- 免疫检查点分子（如PD-1, CTLA-4, LAG-3）在稳态调节中的作用；
- 代谢通路（如mTOR, AMPK, 糖酵解/氧化磷酸化）对免疫细胞分化的影响；
- 组织微环境因素（如缺氧、营养、胞外基质）对免疫细胞功能的调节；
- 人类遗传变异（如SNPs）对免疫稳态相关基因表达和功能的影响；
- 跨物种（小鼠vs人类）和跨组织（淋巴器官vs屏障组织vs肿瘤）的保守性与差异性。

必须排除或降权的内容

- 只报告差异表达基因、未进行功能验证或机制探索的纯组学描述性文章；
- 仅使用一种永生化细胞系，且没有原代细胞或体内验证的研究；
- 与免疫稳态和调节无直接关联的普通微生物学、肿瘤生长曲线或药物化学论文；
- 没有使用人类样本或人道动物模型验证的纯粹计算模拟论文。

DeepSurvey 风格知识构建

每篇入库论文除 method-scenario-benchmark 外，必须提取：

keynote:
  core_problem: 该论文解决的具体免疫学问题（如Treg功能不稳定性、IL-2阈值效应）
  experimental_system: 体外/体内、小鼠/人类、健康/疾病、组织来源
  causal_chains:
    - trigger: 干预（如细胞因子添加、基因敲除、药物处理）或自然实验（如遗传变异）
      steps:
        - claim: 中介机制（如信号通路激活、转录因子表达、代谢转变）
          evidence: 论文中的来源摘录
          evidence_type: experimental | theoretical | observational | inferred
      outcome: 细胞功能（增殖、凋亡、细胞因子产生）、亚群频率、疾病表型
      observables: 流式细胞术、RNA-seq、ATAC-seq、代谢物、细胞因子浓度
      interventions: 可操纵变量（如抗IL-2抗体、雷帕霉素）
  experimental_evidence: 样本数、实验条件、对照和主要终点
  assumptions: 隐含假设（如体外激活状态代表体内）、可迁移条件和未测混杂
  limitations: 作者明确局限（如体外模型、小样本量、特定品系小鼠）
  important_claims: 声明与证据的一一对应
  future_directions: 明确未来工作
  code_or_implementation: 公开代码、模型版本、分析环境；不得下载或处理患者敏感数据

## TanXi：Gap 探索要求

TanXi 首先做因果断链检测，而不是把方法尚未用于某细胞亚群作为高价值 Gap。

优先发现以下问题：

1. 遗传变异 → 细胞内在状态 → 细胞因子敏感性 → 稳态增殖/凋亡 中，哪一段缺乏可测量中介、干预证据或跨模型验证；
2. 某个表面标志物或转录因子究竟是稳态维持的驱动者，还是仅仅是旁观者或激活后的附属现象；
3. 稳态调节在不同组织微环境（如淋巴器官、屏障组织、炎症部位、肿瘤）之间是否存在根本性不同，而非仅是细胞数量差异；
4. 体外实验中的信号阈值是否在体内复杂的细胞因子网络和细胞-细胞接触中被噪声淹没，导致预测失效；
5. Treg与Teff之间的动态平衡，在特定扰动（如感染、自身免疫）下，其恢复的稳态点是唯一的还是多稳态的；
6. 免疫系统的冗余性如何掩盖核心调节节点的作用，使得单个基因敲除无表型，但组合干预或特定背景下才显现；
7. 从小鼠到人类，哪些调节机制是保守的，哪些是基于特定寿命、环境暴露和免疫经历而特有的；
8. 衰老、肥胖、性别等个体间差异如何修改稳态调节的基线和响应规则。

每个 ranked gap 必须输出：

mechanism_draft:
  input: 已定义的扰动（如感染、自身抗原、免疫治疗）或个体差异变量
  proposed_mediator: 仅可填写文献来源支持的中介；否则保持 unresolved
  output: 明确的细胞功能、细胞群体动态或系统状态（如炎症消退、自身耐受维持）
  source_clue: 原文 citation 与 excerpt
  causal_gap:
    missing_kind: intermediate_mechanism | observability | intervention |
                  counterfactual | evidence_window
    evidence_needed: 下一步需要何种直接证据
  sub_hypothesis_id: SH1 | SH2 | SH3 | SH4
  unresolved_fields:
    - identity
    - location_or_scope
    - dynamics
    - reversibility
    - observability
    - intervention
    - counterfactual

禁止把某组学技术尚未用于某疾病状态某新发现的细胞亚群功能未知直接列为核心 Gap。

## Socrates：机制合同与因果补全

只选择排名第一、且属于机制型或矛盾型的 Gap 建立机制合同。

Socrates evidence contract:
  identity: 中介实体/过程到底是什么（如IL-2/IL-2R信号强度、TCR亲和力、Treg特异性转录因子Foxp3的表达水平）
  location_or_scope: 位于哪类细胞（如CD4+ Foxp3+ Treg）、组织（如淋巴结、肠道、肿瘤微环境）或时间窗（如急性期vs恢复期）
  dynamics: 如何随扰动强度、时间进程或局部细胞因子浓度变化
  reversibility: 是否可逆，何种条件下可恢复（如撤除刺激、输入外源性细胞因子）
  observability: 如何直接测量（如流式、单细胞测序、体内成像），而不是只测终末分化标志物
  intervention: 什么变量可在实验伦理和技术上被操纵（如细胞因子中和、基因编辑、过继转移）
  counterfactual: 若抑制/增强/移除该中介，预期细胞群体动态和系统稳态发生什么变化

每个字段必须有：

evidence:
  - citation: 真实论文、综述或实验研究来源
    excerpt: 支持该字段的原文摘录
    paper_id: PaperGraph记录
    evidence_type: experimental | theoretical | observational | inferred

Socrates 必须同时输出：

- 反事实或准实验设计（如使用IL-2突变体只结合特定受体、Foxp3条件性敲除、使用报告小鼠追踪细胞命运）；
- 第一性原理推导待补项，例如受体占有模型对Treg存活的影响、IL-2消耗速率与Treg扩增动力学的关系；
- 竞争机制消除计划（如区分Treg内在Foxp3缺失 vs. 通过TGF-β或IL-10的旁观者效应）；
- 哪些证据来自人类原代细胞、小鼠模型或体外系统，禁止混淆证据层级。

若 verdict != COMPLETE，输出 INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE、剩余字段和定向检索建议，停止 MingLi 假设生成；不得用常识、临床直觉或模板补全。

## MingLi：假设生成要求

MingLi 只可消费：

- Socrates COMPLETE 的机制合同；
- 对应子假设满足 P0–P2 证据窗口；
- 有明确细胞类型、扰动范围和可观测终点的 Gap。

每条假设必须包含：

hypothesis:
  sub_hypothesis_id: SHx
  cell_and_tissue_scope: 细胞亚群、组织部位、生理或病理状态
  perturbation_scope: 特定的体外刺激、体内扰动或遗传变异
  causal_chain: X → Z → Y
  X: 可干预变量或可定义的自然实验（如外源性IL-2浓度、抗原剂量、基因多态性）
  Z: 直接可测量中介（如pSTAT5水平、Foxp3表达、线粒体膜电位）
  Y: 细胞功能（增殖、凋亡、细胞因子产生）、群体频率（Treg/Teff比例）或系统状态（炎症评分）
  operating_conditions:
    - 特定细胞因子组合、TCR刺激强度
    - 氧浓度、代谢底物可用性
    - 细胞-细胞接触（如DC/T细胞共培养）
  observables:
    - 细胞频率（流式细胞术）
    - 信号蛋白磷酸化（磷酸化流式）
    - 转录组和表观组（scRNA-seq/scATAC-seq）
    - 代谢物（Seahorse、质谱）
    - 细胞因子浓度（Luminex/MSD）
  null_hypothesis: 不存在经中介 Z 传递的调节效应，或该效应在体内被冗余/补偿机制掩盖
  alternative_explanations:
    - 细胞外因素的间接影响（如其他细胞类型产生的细胞因子）
    - 非细胞自主性机制（如Treg通过DC间接影响Teff）
    - 系统冗余（如多个信号通路同时调节）
    - 观察到的相关性纯属伴随状态（如激活状态）
  falsification_condition: 预注册的失败条件（如Z未改变或Y不变、方向相反、阻断X后Y不恢复）
  source_mapping: 每个因果链步骤对应 Socrates citation/excerpt

合格假设的形式应类似：

> 在定义明确的体外分化体系或体内扰动模型中，若通过 X 改变信号强度，并直接测得中介 Z 的预期变化，而 Y 在匹配对照下按预注册方向改变，则支持该机制；若 Z 未改变、Y 与 Z 脱钩，或竞争机制更能解释数据，则否定或修订假设。

禁止使用免疫组学数据可指导治疗通过研究Treg可理解免疫耐受之类没有机制实体和证伪条件的万能模板。

## YanZhen：两次机制审计

初审

- 内部一致性：变量、细胞范围、扰动范围、时间顺序和中介定义是否一致；
- 数据一致性：citation/excerpt 是否真正支持所声称的细胞类型、扰动和终点；
- 因果一致性：是否把相关误当因果关系，是否遗漏关键混杂（如细胞频率依赖的比例效应）；
- 体外与体内区分：体外实验的发现不能直接外推为体内稳态机制；
- 输出：unsupported_claims、required_actions、verdict。

至少两个 regime-shift 检验

regime_shift_1:
  condition: 从健康个体到自身免疫病或肿瘤微环境
  question: 调节机制（如IL-2依赖性）是否仍然成立，或是否被微环境中的抑制/增强信号覆盖

regime_shift_2:
  condition: 从体外细胞培养到体内复杂环境（如静脉注射vs.局部组织）
  question: 信号阈值或反馈控制是否因细胞-细胞接触、基质粘附或药代动力学差异而改变

regime_shift_3:
  condition: 从小鼠模型到人类原代细胞/人体样本
  question: 关键中介分子（如Foxp3, IL-2R）的表达和功能是否保守，或是否存在物种特异性差异

regime_shift_4:
  condition: 从短期急性刺激到长期慢性炎症或衰老
  question: 调节机制是否存在时间依赖性转变，如从稳态维持到功能衰竭

若初审不是 PASS，YanZhen 必须把具体失败点反馈给 MingLi 和 DuZhi；不得直接输出成功结论。

## DuZhi + BianLun：3–5 轮结构化辩论

DuZhi 每轮必须针对上一轮的具体回应追问，并至少覆盖：

1. 免疫稳态的操作定义是什么：是特定细胞频率、细胞因子浓度、信号强度，还是整个系统的弹性（resilience）？
2. 观察到的Treg/Teff比值变化，是稳态调节的驱动者，还是响应者（如由抗原负荷或炎症水平驱动）？
3. 中介变量 Z 如何直接测量，并区分于下游效应？例如pSTAT5是信号转导中介，还是仅仅是激活标记？
4. 若关键基因敲除（如Foxp3）在不同遗传背景或微生物群条件下表型严重度不同，机制是否仍然普遍成立？
5. 体外实验中确定的信号阈值（如IL-2浓度），在体内因细胞因子沉没或局部浓度梯度而无法实现时，该机制是否仍有生理意义？
6. 不同组织（如淋巴结vs.肠道）中，Treg的功能和稳定性是否由同一套调节逻辑控制，还是需独立建模？
7. 所谓的稳态点是单一吸引子，还是一个动态变化范围；如果是后者，如何定义可操作的重平衡目标？

BianLun 每轮必须输出：

round_summary:
  resolved_disputes: []
  unresolved_disputes: []
  causal_links_requiring_revision: []
  evidence_missing: []
  required_mingli_revision: []

MingLi 必须保留修订前后版本、修改理由、引用变化和仍未解决的不确定性。

## 最终交付物与安全边界

最终输出必须包括：

- Objective Decomposition、每个 SH 的状态和组合结论门槛；
- P0–P4 文献覆盖、子空间覆盖、来源质量和预印本缺口报告；
- PaperGraph 因果证据图与断链位置；
- TanXi ranked gaps 与 mechanism_draft；
- Socrates evidence contract、反事实设计、第一性原理待补项；
- 初始假设、3–5 轮 DuZhi 质询、BianLun 归纳、MingLi 修订轨迹；
- YanZhen 初审与复审；
- 最终状态：ACCEPT_FOR_EXPERIMENT、CONDITIONAL_ACCEPT、REJECTED 或 REQUIRES_HUMAN_REVIEW；
- 可证伪的验证设计，包括体外/体内实验系统、扰动方案、对照设置、混杂控制、统计功效分析和数据共享要求；
- 跨物种、跨组织、跨年龄/性别/遗传背景的可迁移性评估；
- 未解决证据缺口、不能外推的细胞类型/组织/疾病状态，以及后续定向检索建议。

所有涉及人类原代细胞或动物实验的方案，都只能作为研究设计输出；实际执行必须经过相应的机构审查委员会（IRB/IACUC）或伦理审查，并遵守相关生物安全和数据治理规定。

1. 特定免疫细胞亚群（如Treg, Th17, CD8+ T）的稳态增殖、分化与凋亡动态；
2. 关键细胞因子或代谢信号通路（如IL-2, TGF-β, mTOR）的阈值效应与反馈调节；
3. 免疫调节干预（如低剂量IL-2、检查点激动剂/拮抗剂）是否能够重建而非简单地增强或抑制免疫应答；
4. 上述稳态调节规则在不同遗传背景、组织微环境（屏障组织vs.循环系统）、年龄、炎症状态及跨物种（小鼠模型vs.人类）迁移时是否仍成立。

sub_hypotheses.focus: Conservation of Treg function across species
controls: Intra-species controls (e.g., different mouse strains, human cohorts)
sub_hypotheses.falsification_condition: Significant differences in Treg function or immune homeostasis between species
sub_hypotheses.retrieval_query: Treg function species conservation immune homeostasis
query_variants: Treg function species conservation immune homeostasis

evidence_window.P0_latest_preprint: Recent preprints on Treg function in different species
evidence_window.P1_recent_primary: Primary studies on Treg function in mice and humans within the last two years
evidence_window.P2_mechanism_or_theory: Mechanistic studies on Treg function and stability across species

sub_hypotheses.source_objective:
在定义明确的生理状态、扰动条件（如感染、自身抗原、肿瘤）和干预窗口下，个体的遗传背景、细胞组成、细胞内在状态、局部微环境（如细胞因子、代谢物）以及外部扰动，是否能够以可重复、可量化、因果上可解释的方式预测：
1. 特定免疫细胞亚群（如Treg, Th17, CD8+ T）的稳态增殖、分化与凋亡动态；
2. 关键细胞因子或代谢信号通路（如IL-2, TGF-β, mTOR）的阈值效应与反馈调节；
3. 免疫调节干预（如低剂量IL-2、检查点激动剂/拮抗剂）是否能够重建而非简单地增强或抑制免疫应答；
4. 上述稳态调节规则在不同遗传背景、组织微环境（屏障组织vs.循环系统）、年龄、炎症状态及跨物种（小鼠模型vs.人类）迁移时是否仍成立。

combination_hypothesis.statement: The combined effect of IL-2 signaling, mTOR pathway, low-dose IL-2 administration, and species-specific Treg function can predict and reconstruct immune homeostasis in a quantifiable and causal manner.
combination_hypothesis.integration_test: Test that distinguishes additive from coupled effects by comparing the combined impact of manipulating all independent variables versus each independently.

retrieval: Use semantic_scholar, PubMed, bioRxiv/medRxiv; clearly label preprints as not peer-reviewed. Import at least 15 quality-screened papers covering each sub-hypothesis. Exclude descriptive omics papers without functional validation, single immortalized cell line studies, and purely computational papers without human or animal model validation.
gap_detection: Identify gaps in measurable intermediates, interventions, or cross-model validations. Prioritize gaps in the causal chain from genetic variation to immune homeostasis, and in the translatability of in vitro findings to in vivo systems.
hypothesis: Generate hypotheses only from Socrates COMPLETE mechanism contracts. Each hypothesis must have a clear cell type, perturbation range, and observable endpoint. Include a null hypothesis, alternative explanations, and falsification conditions.
verification: Audit hypotheses for internal, data, and causal consistency. Perform regime-shift tests to assess the robustness of the mechanisms across different physiological states, tissues, and species. Final output must include a detailed verification design, including experimental systems, perturbation schemes, and statistical power analysis.

decomposition_rules: Each sub-hypothesis must expose an intervention, observable outcome, falsification condition, and evidence window.
A combined conclusion is admissible only after the relevant component hypotheses are independently evaluated.
Missing quantitative bounds remain explicitly unresolved; they must not be fabricated from the objective.

edges.evidence_excerpt: This observation indicates that, at homeostasis, there is a balance between Tregs and effector T cells that prevents severe inflammation and autoimmunity. This, in turn, teaches us that any chronic breach of this homeostasis, that is, an autoimmune condition, inherently signals Treg insufficiency.1

steps.claim: IL-2 is dispensable for maintaining lineage stability in mature Tregs.
steps.evidence: In contrast to Treg development, we find that IL-2 is dispensable for maintaining lineage stability in mature Tregs.

steps.claim: Continuous IL-2 signaling is needed for long-term Treg survival.
steps.evidence: Although continuous IL-2 signaling is needed for long-term Treg survival, CD25-deleted Tregs may persist for several weeks in vivo using IL-7.

steps.claim: Defects in glycolytic metabolism and suppressor function occur following CD25 deletion.
steps.evidence: We also observe defects in glycolytic metabolism and suppressor function following CD25 deletion.

chains.outcome: Mature Tregs require continuous IL-2 signaling to maintain survival and suppressor function, but not to maintain lineage stability.
observables: Persistence of CD25-deleted Tregs in vivo using IL-7

interventions: Inducible deletion of CD25 in Tregs after thymic development

steps.claim: Decrease in Foxp3 expression causes CD4+ T cells to differentiate into Th17 cells instead of Tregs.

chains.outcome: Impaired Treg function and increased Th17 cell differentiation.

steps.claim: Stimulation and expansion of regulatory T cells (Tregs)
steps.evidence: This is in line with the main activity of low-dose IL-2, the stimulation and expansion of regulatory T cells (Tregs).1

steps.claim: Restoration of the balance between Tregs and effector T cells
steps.evidence: This observation indicates that, at homeostasis, there is a balance between Tregs and effector T cells that prevents severe inflammation and autoimmunity. This, in turn, teaches us that any chronic breach of this homeostasis, that is, an autoimmune condition, inherently signals Treg insufficiency.1

steps.claim: Reduction in inflammation and autoimmunity
steps.evidence: Their depletion immediately unleashes major activation of effector T cells leading to fatal inflammation and the attack of otherwise healthy tissues, leading to multiorgan autoimmunity.3

steps.evidence: In vitro, HSA-IL2m induced STAT5 phosphorylation only in Tregs, while HSA-IL2wt induced STAT5 phosphorylation in Tregs, NK, and conventional T cells.

steps.claim: HSA-IL2m leads to sustained Treg expansion and activation in vivo
steps.evidence: In vivo, HSA-IL2m led to sustained Treg expansion and activation in both mice and cynomolgus monkeys, with a prolonged effect compared to HSA-IL2wt.

steps.claim: HSA-IL2m reduces disease severity in mouse models of acute graft versus host disease and experimental autoimmune encephalomyelitis
steps.evidence: Treatment with HSA-IL2m maintained the pool of host B cells, suppressed donor CD8 T cell engraftment, and increased the number of Tregs, leading to reduced disease severity in the mouse models.

references: Suyasha Roy et al. (2025) BLIMP1 negatively regulates IL-2 signaling in T cells doi:10.1126/sciadv.adx8105

evidence_type_annotations.claim: genome-wide CRISPR-knockout screening
BLIMP1 inhibits IL-2 signaling in T cells, both in normal and pathophysiological conditions, indicating potential therapeutic applications.
BLIMP1 inhibits IL-2 production by T cells; however, its role in IL-2 signaling remains unknown.

Deleting PRDM1 in human CD4+ T cells and Treg cells also increased IL-2 signaling. Furthermore, CD4+ T cells from patients with ATL expressed less BLIMP1 and had enhanced IL-2 signaling, whereas overexpressing PRDM1 in ATL cells suppressed IL-2 signaling. Thus, BLIMP1 inhibits IL-2 signaling during...[truncated]

To identify regulators of IL-2 signaling, we performed genome-wide CRISPR-knockout screening in IL-2–dependent cells derived from a patient with adult T cell leukemia (ATL) and found enrichment of single guide RNAs targeting PRDM1, which encodes B lymphocyte–induced maturation protein 1 (BLIMP1).
Thus, BLIMP1 inhibits IL-2 signaling during normal and pathophysiological responses, suggesting that manipulating BLIMP1 could have therapeutic potential.

Continuous IL-2 signaling is required for the survival and suppressor function of mature Tregs, but not for lineage stability.
In contrast to Treg development, we find that IL-2 is dispensable for maintaining lineage stability in mature Tregs.

Although continuous IL-2 signaling is needed for long-term Treg survival, CD25-deleted Tregs may persist for several weeks in vivo using IL-7. We also observe defects in glycolytic metabolism and suppressor function following CD25 deletion. Thus, unlike developing Tregs in which the primary role of...[truncated]

(2018) OX40 Costimulation Inhibits Foxp3 Expression and Treg Induction via BATF3-Dependent and Independent Mechanisms.

Specifically, OX40 upregulates BATF3 and BATF, which produce a closed chromatin configuration to repress Foxp3 expression in a Sirt1/7-dependent manner. Moreover, OX40 can also activate the AKT-mTOR pathway, especially in the absence of BATF3 and BATF, to inhibit Foxp3 induction, and this is mediate...[truncated]
Taken together, our results provide key mechanistic insights into how OX40 inhibits Foxp3 expression and Treg induction in the periphery.

CD226 maintains Treg cell stability and metabolism via the mTOR/Myc pathway under inflammatory conditions, and its deficiency leads to impaired Treg cell function and metabolic changes
Here, we show that CD226 primarily maintains the Treg cell stability and metabolism phenotype under inflammatory conditions.

Treg cell-specific deletion of CD226 increases the Treg cell percentage in immune organs but weakens their immunosuppressive function with a T helper 1-like phenotype conversion under inflammation. CD226-deficient Treg cells exhibit reduced oxidative phosphorylation and increased glycolysis rates, w...[truncated]

evidence_type_annotations.claim: the mtor signaling pathway
Mettl14 is critical for the differentiation and suppressive function of iTregs, acting through the mTOR signaling pathway
However, beyond meeting the epigenetic modification of Treg cells, whether Mettl14 plays a role in the fate determination of iTregs is unclear.

We further checked the altered signaling pathway in METTL14-deficient iTregs. We found that reduced METTL14 leads to activation of the mTOR pathway with increased p-mTOR and p-p70S6K, which are known to modulate the suppressive function of iTregs. In conclusion, our study revealed that Mettl14 plays...[truncated]
Increasing lines of evidence suggest that methyltransferase-like 14 (METTL14), a critical component of the m6A writer complex, regulates the stability and function of the Treg cells.
According to the results, Mettl14 deficiency resulted in the disruption of iTregs differentiation evidenced by the limited FoxP3 expression.
The results of the colitis development in Rag1-/- mice and CFSE assays revealed that loss of METTL14 significantly compromised the suppressive function of iTregs in vivo and in vitro.

evidence_type_annotations.claim: review of studies
Updated overview of the role of Treg impairment in the pathogenesis of autoimmune liver diseases and discussion on potential therapeutic strategies for Treg restoration.
Challenges and caveats remain when implementing strategies for Treg generation/expansion.

Treg defects play a key permissive role in autoimmune disease pathogenesis by favoring overwhelming effector T cell immunity and, consequently, tissue damage exacerbation and progression. Numerical and functional impairment of Tregs and alterations of the associated signaling pathways is pivotal in...[truncated]
Defects in Treg frequency and/or function result in overwhelming CD4 and CD8 T cell immune responses participating in the autoimmune attack.

In this Review, we outline studies reporting the role of Treg impairment in the pathogenesis of these conditions and discuss methods to restore Treg number and function either by generation/expansion in the test tube or through in vivo expansion upon administration of low dose IL-2.

Treg impairment can be also associated with phenotypic and functional plasticity that results in Treg skewing to effector cells; this being regarded as a mechanism enabling the maintenance of proinflammatory T cell pools.
Future studies are warranted to determine the efficacy and the feasibility of these approaches in large patients’ cohorts.

edges.evidence_excerpt: This is in line with the main activity of low-dose IL-2, the stimulation and expansion of regulatory T cells (Tregs).1

edges.citation: D. Klatzmann (2019) Response to: ‘Circulating regulatory T cells were absolutely decreased in dermatomyositis/polymyositis patients and restored by low-dose IL-2’ by Zhang et al doi:10.1136/annrheumdis-2019-216267
edges.evidence_excerpt: This observation indicates that, at homeostasis, there is a balance between Tregs and effector T cells that prevents severe inflammation and autoimmunity. This, in turn, teaches us that any chronic breach of this homeostasis, that is, an autoimmune condition, inherently signals Treg insufficiency.1

edges.evidence_excerpt: Their depletion immediately unleashes major activation of effector T cells leading to fatal inflammation and the attack of otherwise healthy tissues, leading to multiorgan autoimmunity.3
